industry: Pyrography — wood burning art, tools, and tutorials
target_audience: Hobbyist and intermediate pyrography artists aged 25-55, DIY craft enthusiasts, woodworking hobbyists, people looking to start a new creative skill, gift makers, and small craft business owners who sell burned wood art
tone_of_voice: Friendly, passionate, and knowledgeable — like advice from an experienced friend who loves pyrography. Encouraging for beginners, detailed enough for intermediate artists.
content_pillars: Tool reviews and comparisons — wood burning pens, tips, shading tools
How-to guides and tutorials — step by step techniques for beginners and intermediate artists
Wood selection guides — best wood types, grain, preparation, sealing
Pattern and design ideas — animals, landscapes, mandalas, portraits, lettering
Common mistakes and how to fix them
Project showcases — finished pieces, before and after
Product recommendations — safety gear, transferring tools, finishes
Community engagement — asking followers to share their work
call_to_action_style: Drive traffic to website for full tutorials, ask followers to share their work in comments, encourage saves for reference
hashtag_strategy: Mix of broad craft tags and niche pyrography tags — always include #pyrography #woodburning #pyrowithjared
